scenario: Wagtail work session - audit every production subsystem that depends on the page model before reworking its contract

map_quality.rationale: Names Wagtail as a Django CMS, splits the backend into models/actions/admin/api/permissions/contrib/serving cleanly, and pins the page model to `wagtail/models/pages.py:289` as the tree node — a downstream agent can act with tiny follow-ups.

specificity.rationale: Concrete paths throughout (`wagtail/models/pages.py:289`, `wagtail/actions/`, `wagtail/admin/`, `wagtail/contrib/{redirects,search_promotions,...}`) and the exact class signature `class Page(AbstractPage, index.Indexed, ClusterableModel, ...)` are cited.

justification.rationale: Explains WHY Page matters ("tree-node model editors create/move/publish/serve", "central hub, 148 callers / 250 references") rather than just naming it, though the reasoning is a single sentence.

uncertainty.rationale: No explicit uncertainty flagging on the classification of subsystems or on the caller counts; the answer is confident throughout, which is defensible at this orientation step but leaves no hedges for the agent.

map_quality.rationale: Answer maps all four contract layers with file:line — tree mechanics from MP_Node/AbstractPage (path/depth/numchild), publishing state from DraftStateMixin (live/has_unpublished_changes/etc), alias mechanics (alias_of at :392), and the three modes of reference-holding (FK, generic ct+id, transient queries). Missing an explicit call-out of specific/specific_deferred mechanics (the polymorphic dispatch to subclass) which is a load-bearing part of the contract.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every field/method/manager is anchored to a concrete file:line — 'title :290', 'slug :298', 'alias_of :392', 'BasePageManager at wagtail/models/pages.py:144', 'DraftStateMixin live:14', 'ReferenceIndex at wagtail/models/reference_index.py:119'. Only 'get_descendants/get_children' are named without individual line refs, but they're identified as MP_Node inherited.

justification.rationale: The opening frame ('most of the "page" fields are not declared on Page itself — they come from mixins... which is exactly what makes a field-relocation risky') and the closing 'how other code holds page references (the crux)' with FK/generic/transient tiering directly tell the agent restructuring order follows coupling strength. The search_fields note ('the search index is coupled to these field names') is a concrete risk pointer.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer is asserted with confidence throughout; nothing is flagged as unverified even though several claims (e.g. exact line numbers on inherited mixins) could be stale. No caveats about pieces the tool couldn't confirm.

steps.step: Enumerate every production dependent of the page model

map_quality.rationale: The list is broad and covers the scattered tail the audience section flags — site root FK (models/sites.py:108), search promotions (contrib/search_promotions/models.py:104), dashboard/side panels (admin/ui/side_panels.py:268), reports (admin/views/reports/locked_pages.py, page_types_usage.py), template tag (wagtailcore_tags.py:52), chooser (admin/views/chooser.py), and API. Some expected surfaces (e.g. dashboard 'Your pages'/'Recent edits' panels, listing/explorer views, admin urls, workflows tasks) are not obviously represented, leaving a few gaps.

specificity.rationale: Every entry is a concrete file:line with the specific symbol named in a trailing comment; nothing is collapsed into 'various admin views' and one-file/many-dependents is expanded (e.g. move_page.py:35 and :99, chooser.py:235/440/512).

justification.rationale: The list stays on production dependents of the contract — no tests or migrations sneak in — and the comments identify the coupling kind (FK, ParentalKey, action, view) without editorializing, matching the step's 'flat list only' instruction.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer never flags coverage limits or areas it could not fully verify; given the audience emphasizes completeness and the list is likely partial (e.g. urls/dashboard panels), silent omission is a notable weakness.

map_quality.rationale: Comprehensive subsystem grouping covering serving, API (public+admin), actions, admin UI (choosers/forms/reports/panels), permissions, management commands, i18n, satellite FK models, reference index, cache, sitemaps, and signals — each with coupling kind stated ('stores a page FK', 'reads live/locked', 'walks tree via get_descendants').

specificity.rationale: Every entry carries concrete file:line anchors (e.g. 'models/sites.py:108 Site.root_page FK', 'permission_policies/pages.py:162/177', 'search_promotions/models.py:104') — no vague 'various admin views' collapses.

justification.rationale: Grouping surfaces the shape of the change (FK-embedding vs querying vs signal-driven vs tree-mutating), and the 'Satellite models that persist a page reference' bucket cleanly separates schema-coupling risk from transient querying.

uncertainty.rationale: No explicit flags on uncertain subsystem assignments or gaps — e.g. the 'Generic reference tracking' item is noted structurally but not called out as an uncertain-boundary case, and no acknowledgment of possible missed subsystems.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: Cleanly separates schema-embedding dependents (Site.root_page, Redirect, GroupPagePermission, WorkflowPage, SearchPromotion, form submissions) from transient queriers (serve/route, APIs, actions, permission policy), and names the driving editor/visitor flow for most tiers. Tier 3 collapses several dependents into a single line without individual flow attribution, which slightly weakens the map.

specificity.rationale: Tier 1 and 2 entries carry concrete file:line anchors (e.g. `models/sites.py:108`, `contrib/redirects/models.py:33`, `views.py:16`, `permission_policies/pages.py:162`). Tier 3 groups 'choosers, reports, side panels, sitemaps' without file:line, leaving the agent to re-locate them.

justification.rationale: Each risk claim ties to coupling kind ('FK-coupled', 'ParentalKey-coupled', 'reads live + tree/url_path') and product impact ('all serving breaks', 'silently opens or closes access'). The ranking argument is coherent — schema-embedding first, hot-path transients next, feature-scoped last.

uncertainty.rationale: The reference_index caveat ('silently under/over-reports') is flagged as a judgment call, but the tier assignments and 'break a whole user class silently' claims are asserted without labeling them as inferences. No mention of unverified assumptions in Tier 3's collapsed list.

map_quality.rationale: Surfaces genuinely non-obvious dependents across signal wiring (frontend_cache), transitive tree reach (sitemaps), generic content_type references (reference_index, viewsets), rich-text embeds, locales gate, template tags, and named-constant coupling. Missing the classic 'lazy string FK' examples like sites.py root_page and search_promotions that the rubric explicitly calls out, though it does capture equivalent hard-to-grep patterns.

specificity.rationale: Most entries carry concrete file:line (e.g. `signal_handlers.py:7-23`, `reference_index.py:119`, `viewsets/pages.py:583-615`, `models/pages.py:2674`). A couple are looser (`admin/rich_text/converters/*` wildcards, `wagtail/signals.py:15/18` compound refs) but the core anchors are usable.

justification.rationale: Every entry explains exactly why grep misses it: 'reached purely through signal wiring', 'one hop removed from any Page import', 'holds page references generically via content_type', 'expressed through a named constant, not a literal field name'. The 'why easy to miss' explanations map faithfully to the reference style.

uncertainty.rationale: No explicit flagging of blind spots or unverified claims — the answer states each finding as fact without acknowledging that the search could still miss other lazy FKs, string-referenced hooks, or config-driven dependents.

map_quality.rationale: The map is comprehensive: satellite FK models, generic ReferenceIndex, serving/routing, API, actions, admin UI, permissions, management commands, locales, signals, sitemaps, and non-obvious dependents are all grouped by subsystem with per-dependent verification notes (e.g., 'verify path/depth/numchild/live/aliases writes' for actions, 'verify signal senders still fire' for frontend cache). Nothing from earlier steps appears dropped.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every entry carries a concrete file:line (e.g., `wagtail/models/sites.py:108`, `wagtail/actions/move_page.py:35/99`, `wagtail/admin/viewsets/pages.py:583/601/609`). Both the table and the JSON dependents list are anchored — a teammate can navigate directly without re-exploration.

justification.rationale: Each verification note follows from the coupling kind ('FK → root-path cache, serving'; 'reads locked'; 'tree-ancestry checks; verify after any path/depth change'). The highest_risk section argues from coupling and product impact (visitor serving, silent access-control failure).

uncertainty.rationale: The method note explicitly states the tools used and no sub-agents, which bounds the search, but there is no explicit 'residual unknowns' or 'blind spots' follow-up list — uncertainty is only implicitly hinted at rather than called out as explicit TODOs.
